content:
Hi all,

I’ve run out of quota on Gmail and my other mailbox, so while sending patches via b4 works, I can’t reliably reply to threads.

Given that, I’m choosing between only two options:

  1. Extending / reusing the b4 relay to send replies (with proper In-Reply-To / References), not just patch uploads.

    • Has anyone done this or looked at the relevant b4 code paths?

  2. Running my own SMTP server on DigitalOcean (I have some balance left).

    • Any known issues with DO + kernel mailing lists? Port blocks, deliverability, required SPF/DKIM setup, etc.?

Is self-hosted SMTP the only reliable long-term way here, or is option (1) viable in practice?

You can get a linux.dev email and use that for linux development:
https://korg.docs.kernel.org/linuxdev.html
What's the daily limit on Gmail? Maybe they just block burst mails temporarily rather than triggering daily quota?

@Onur Özkan I guess having so many CCs and To in the email count as multiple emails.
I know I reached this limit cause thunderbird was stuck with showing me an endless loader. This may be a API request rate limit too. Not sure.
You are on a custom email. What kind of setup are you on, and how is your experience been like?
I am using yandex service, never hit any issues so far
I don't know if it's still possible but it was free to create custom emails on yandex ~4 years ago

Alice Ryhl said:

You can get a linux.dev email and use that for linux development:
https://korg.docs.kernel.org/linuxdev.html

I will give this a try first then look for other options.
Trying out zohomail. Initial impressions seem fine. Will try out other mail providers if this doesn't work well.
shivamklr has marked this topic as resolved.
